story: On vacation mornings I only get up if coffee awaits. As a kid, my dad and I slipped out before sunrise: 7‑Eleven for his coffee and my hot chocolate and donuts, then the tackle shop for bait, then the reservoir. I loved buying a Styrofoam cup of worms most; neon dough was the backup. That night, the tackle shop’s live‑bait cooler failed, and the clerk tossed all the worms, leaving only neon dough bait on the shelf. Dad nudged me awake; I pulled on mismatched leggings, and we headed for the Ranger, talking about what to grab at the shop.
implausible_knowledge: That night, the tackle shop’s live‑bait cooler failed, and the clerk tossed all the worms, leaving only neon dough bait on the shelf.
question: As they climb into the truck and discuss what to pick up at the tackle shop, what will she ask her dad to buy?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask her dad to skip worms and get neon dough bait instead.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask her dad to buy a Styrofoam cup of worms.
